More Notes: Climate suitability for dengue transmission has expanded in North America
Climate suitability for dengue transmission has expanded in East Asia
Climate suitability for dengue transmission has expanded in the Mediterranean basin
Increase largely driven by population growth in areas with historically favorable climate suitability
Increase predominantly occurred in previously unfavorable areas with limited population growth
Population in areas with high climate suitability has grown by approximately 2.5 billion.
Approximately 70% of the populations live in areas with high risk of infection.

The long-term climate suitability trends for dengue virus transmission from 1979 to 2022 were analyzed using the seasonal Mann–Kendall (MK) test. This analysis included estimates of change by calculating the average Index P for 1979–1983 and 2018–2022, demonstrating climate impacts over time. Value is arbitrarily set to zero since the actual value is not specified.

The independent effect of global population growth in areas with high dengue climate suitability was estimated by holding climate suitability constant between 1979-1983 and allowing population changes from 1980 to 2020, showcasing impacts due to population dynamics. Value is arbitrarily set to zero since the actual value is not specified.

The independent effect of changes in climate suitability on the population living in high suitability areas was estimated by maintaining a constant world population from 1980 and allowing climate suitability to change from 1979 to 2022, indicating climate impacts on population exposure. Value is arbitrarily set to zero since the actual value is not specified.
